BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Back-of-napkin with Jake before the fall lease renewal, to see how much wholesale we need on top of the taproom to cover fixed costs.

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only. Revisit with the accountant once the 2026 books close.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $4,500/mo at fall lease renewal
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs covered by taproom leave ~$4,500 gap; at ~$470/wholesale account, need ~10 accounts to break even. We run 20.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Comfortable headroom at 20 wholesale accounts.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - deep winter taproom dip plus rent bump
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Tight but positive. Lean on can releases and the holiday party to bridge the winter low.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even is ~10 wholesale accounts on top of a normal taproom month. At 20 accounts we have real headroom; a deep winter taproom dip is the thing to watch.
ActionItems: Confirm renewal rent with Burnside Industrial before the October lease deadline
Revisit after year-end with the accountant for proper 2026 modeling
